info.title: Primary JTBD
info.content: Information about Primary JTBD will go here. This is placeholder content that you can replace with your detailed explanation.

info.title: Desired Automation Type
info.content:
There are four types of automation that systems can provide:

Acquisition automation

Description: Acquisition automation means the system gathers data by itself, like human sensing.

Example:
Humans: we notice things through our senses (vision, hearing, touch, etc)
Systems: they can sense automatically.
e.g Sensors automatically collect temperature, pressure or movement data.

Analysis automation

Description: Analysis automation means the system analyses data automatically, like human thinking and interpreting.

Example:
Humans: we process what we've sensed, spotting patterns, remembering similar situations, and making sense of information.
Systems: they can also interpret what they collect.
e.g Calculating averages, detecting a spike, spotting trends.

Decision automation

Description: Decision automation means the system makes the decision, like human choice-making.

Example:
Humans: we decide what should happen next after interpreting information.
Systems: they can also choose an action based on what the data suggests.
e.g Deciding that a machine needs cooling because the temperature is too high.

Action automation

Description: Action automation means the system executes the chosen action, like human physical response.

Example:
Humans: once we decide, we act (move, press a button, switch something off, etc)
Systems: they can also carry out the action themselves.

info.title: Desired Automation Level
info.content:
The automation level describes the degree of autonomy the system has in decision-making and action-taking:

  1. The system offers no assistance: the human must take all decisions and actions
  2. The system offers a complete set of decisions or action alternatives.
  3. The system narrows the selection of alternative actions down to a few.
  4. The system suggests one alternative action
  5. The system makes a decision to perform automatic execution if the human approves
  6. The system allows the human a restricted time to veto before automatic execution
  7. The system executes automatically then necessarily informs the human.
  8. The system informs the human only if asked.
  9. The system informs the human only if it, the computer, decides to.
  10. The system decides everything, acts autonomously, ignoring the human.

info.title: Current User AI Maturity
info.content:
AI maturity represents the level of sophistication in how users interact with AI systems:

Learning to drive

AI control: Low

Customer experience: Orientating & appreciation
User understands basic capabilities and boundaries. Focus on simple, accurate tasks where danger/risk is minimal.

Example feature type: e.g Simple summarisation, basic data clean up.

Supervised driving

AI control: Moderate

Customer experience: Human in the loop
AI proposes a starting point; the user corrects, refines, or approves it. This is a critical stage for personalisation and building calibrated trust.

Example feature type: e.g Draft generation (w/ user edits), complex sorting (user reviews before next action).

Co-pilot

AI control: High

Customer experience: AI suggests, human decides
AI operates proactively, suggesting complex actions or insights. The user's role is to accept or reject the high quality suggestion.

Example feature type: e.g Advanced workflow automation suggestions, predictive data insights.

Autonomous driving

AI control: Full

Customer experience: Unsupervised mode
AI makes suggestions and executes decisions or performs complex, multi-step actions on the user's behalf. Requires near-perfect trust from the user.

Example feature type: e.g Fully automated report generation, tax obligation population and submission.
